description.en: Timeweavers are a walking paradox. They are someone who treads the fragile boundary between literal seconds, snatching back moments before they vanish. They manipulate tempo through spells like Borrowed Seconds, exchanging a piece of their vitality for an instant burst of power, while Fragile Tempo turns stress into a fragile second chance. Time itself is slippery and unexplainable - impossible to fully grasp or trust - and that leaves them spectacularly potent, and just as spectacularly vulnerable. A Timeweaver's magic is raw, urgent, and fleeting… dancing at the edge of time itself.
**Spend 3 Hope** after you roll but before outcomes resolve. Reroll the action and keep the new result. If you succeed with Hope, **clear 1 Stress**.

Once per scene when you succeed on a Spellcast Roll against an adversary, you can **mark 1 Hit Point** to immediately make a second Spellcast Roll against a different target within the same range band using the same card. If either roll critically succeeds, **mark 1 Stress**. This does not bypass any 'once per rest' limits printed on that card.
While your Stress is 3 or more, you gain +1 to Spellcast Rolls and −1 Evasion.
